D5 Design L2 Understand Reference

Warm-Cool Contrast

Itten placed orange-red at the warm pole and blue-green at the cool — set two hues and read their felt temperatures

Colour has no physical temperature, but it carries one by convention. Itten fixed orange-red as the warmest hue and blue-green as the coolest, the two poles of an expressive axis. Every other hue projects somewhere between, and a warm beside a cool advances against a receding.

Pick two hues. Each is placed on the warm-cool axis by its projection toward the orange pole; the swatches and the gap between the markers show the warm-cool contrast. This is Itten's convention, not a measurement.
